title: TypeScript Best Practices – Write Clean & Maintainable Code

meta.title: TypeScript Best Practices – Clean Code Guide (2026)
meta.description: Learn TypeScript best practices for writing clean, maintainable, and type-safe code. Includes style guides, patterns, and common pitfalls to avoid.

content.value: TypeScript best practices help you write code that is type-safe, maintainable, and scalable. Following these guidelines reduces bugs, improves collaboration, and makes refactoring easier.
Use Optional Chaining and Nullish Coalescing

rows: Use strict mode, unknown, type guards

value: **Using as type assertions unnecessarily** - Bypasses type checking
**Using enums for runtime values** - Prefer union types or as const
**Ignoring TypeScript errors** - Fix them, don't suppress them

content.value: Following TypeScript best practices leads to safer, more maintainable code. Enable strict mode, avoid any, use proper naming conventions, and leverage TypeScript's powerful type system. Remember: good TypeScript code is not just about making the compiler happy—it's about making your code understandable and reliable.

faq.question: Interface or type alias?
faq.answer: Prefer interfaces for objects; use types for unions, intersections, and mapped types.

faq.question: How to avoid any?
faq.answer: Use unknown, generics, or proper type definitions. Enable noImplicitAny.

faq.question: What is strict mode?
faq.answer: Enables full type checking including null checks, no implicit any, and strict function types.

faq.question: When to use as const?
faq.answer: When you need immutable, literal types for arrays or objects.

quiz.question: What should you use instead of 'any' for unknown types?
Which is preferred for defining object shapes?
